title: CEO Interview with Mohit Gupta of TYLsemi - Semiwiki
gist: TYLsemi, out of stealth in July 2026 with a $43M oversubscribed seed, is positioning as the first pure-play merchant chiplet company (IO, power, memory) targeting custom AI XPUs, claiming ~50% cuts in AI-silicon development time and cost, backed by a TSMC foundry partnership and founders from Alphawave/OpenFive.

insights.claim: TYLsemi argues no pure-play chiplet company currently serves the custom AI-silicon market with a full portfolio, positioning itself to fill that gap with standards-based IO, power and memory chiplets plus UCIe die-to-die connectivity.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Identifies a white-space between hyperscaler-captive chiplets, merchant vendors' self-serving roadmaps and one-off design-service firms; a new merchant category to watch.
insights.quote: yet there is no pure-play chiplet company serving this market with a full portfolio

insights.claim: TYLsemi frames chiplets as 'the new form factor of physical IP' for custom silicon, implying reusable pre-validated dies replace bespoke block-level design.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A mental model reframing chiplets as the successor to the IP-licensing business, relevant to how Synopsys/Cadence/Arm IP value migrates to silicon building blocks.
insights.quote: Chiplets are the new form factor of physical IP for developing custom silicon.

insights.claim: TYLsemi differentiates on delivering real KGD-screened silicon customers can integrate today rather than design files needing further qualification, built on open standards (UCIe, PCIe, Ethernet) to avoid lock-in.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Product-as-silicon vs IP-as-files is the key competitive wedge; watch whether customers value shipped dies over licensable blocks.
insights.quote: We deliver real silicon: KGD-screened chiplets customers can integrate today, not design files requiring further qualification.

insights.claim: TYLsemi cites the AI accelerator market reaching $604 billion by 2033, with custom-silicon XPUs built for specific hyperscaler workloads as the fastest-growing segment.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A concrete TAM anchor for the custom-XPU thesis; supports demand for chiplet, packaging and die-to-die IP suppliers.
insights.quote: The AI accelerator market is on track to reach $604 billion by 2033

insights.claim: TYLsemi claims its chiplet approach drives down development time and cost for AI infrastructure silicon by nearly 50%.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A quantified value proposition; if credible, it accelerates custom-silicon adoption by mid-tier players who couldn't fund full ground-up designs.
insights.quote: driving down development time and cost for AI infrastructure silicon by nearly 50%

insights.claim: AI systems are shifting from monolithic chips to distributed multi-die architectures as chip size approaches physical limits and connectivity and power become the bottlenecks.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: The reticle-limit/power-wall driver underpinning the entire chiplet and advanced-packaging investment thesis.
insights.quote: AI systems are shifting from monolithic chips to distributed, multi-die architectures as chip size approaches physical limits
insights.locator: What are the barriers to designing custom AI silicon

insights.claim: TYLsemi identifies in-package power delivery as a growing unmet gap, arguing AI energy demands expose the limits of conventional power architectures and no existing vendor offers a purpose-built power-delivery chiplet.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Power delivery as a chiplet is a non-obvious differentiator; its integrated voltage regulator (IVR) chiplet targets a bottleneck rivals underweight.
insights.quote: no existing vendor has a purpose-built chiplet solution for it
insights.locator: What does the competitive landscape look like

insights.claim: TYLsemi's IO chiplet family supports PCIe, ESUN and UALink today with a co-packaged optics roadmap for next-generation rack-scale fabrics.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Standards coverage plus a CPO roadmap positions TYLsemi against scale-up interconnect trends (UALink vs NVLink) worth tracking.
insights.quote: supporting PCIe, ESUN, and UALink connectivity today, with a co-packaged optics (CPO) roadmap

insights.claim: TYLsemi is advancing its IO family toward scale-up versions and second-generation 448G speeds.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: 448G SerDes roadmap signals where high-speed die-to-die/scale-up bandwidth is heading.
insights.quote: advancing the TYL.IO family toward scale-up versions and second-generation 448G speeds
insights.locator: What new features/technology are you working on?

insights.claim: TYLsemi came out of stealth in July 2026 alongside an oversubscribed seed round totaling $43 million.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Sizable seed for a chiplet startup reflects investor appetite for custom-AI-silicon enablers.
insights.quote: an oversubscribed seed round with total funding of $43 million

insights.claim: TYLsemi's founders claim to have shipped over 30 million chips, closed over $1B in orders and completed 75+ high-volume designs with first-pass success, plus a 15+ year TSMC relationship.
insights.speaker: Mohit Gupta
insights.so_what: Founder pedigree is the primary underwriting signal for pre-revenue silicon startups; this track record is the credibility basis for the raise.
insights.quote: Our founders have shipped more than 30 million chips, closed over $1B in orders, and completed more than 75 high-volume designs with first-pass success.

insights.claim: CEO Mohit Gupta previously ran the Alphawave Semi custom-silicon/connectivity business acquired by Qualcomm in 2025, and earlier led OpenFive at SiFive, acquired by Alphawave in 2022.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Maps the connectivity-IP M&A lineage (SiFive/OpenFive to Alphawave to Qualcomm) and where the talent recycles into new chiplet ventures.
insights.quote: Alphawave Semi was acquired by Qualcomm in 2025.

insights.claim: TYLsemi secured a foundry partnership with TSMC, which it says recognizes a clear market need for its chiplet portfolio.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A named TSMC partnership is a supply-chain de-risking signal that matters more than product claims at this stage.
insights.quote: we secured a foundry partnership with TSMC, which recognizes a clear need in the market for our chiplet portfolio

insights.claim: TYLsemi says a chip program that slips six months does not just miss a schedule but misses a market window, making time-to-silicon the top customer anxiety in fast-moving AI infrastructure.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Frames time-to-market as the dominant purchasing driver in AI silicon, favoring reuse-based chiplet models over ground-up SoC design.
insights.quote: a program that slips six months does not just miss a schedule; it misses a market window

insights.claim: TYLsemi's Forge platform bundles foundry, OSAT, chiplets and IP under one coordinated program to let it extend from selling standalone chiplets into full customer-defined custom silicon.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: The product-led-into-services model reuses IP and qualification across programs, a potential margin and moat mechanism versus pure design-service firms.
insights.quote: bringing foundry, OSAT, chiplets, and IP under one coordinated program
insights.locator: TYL.Forge was built around these pain points
